record_existence_and_kind.command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "SELECT r.record_id,r.record_kind,r.display_name,p.project_name,p.project_type,p.project_family,p.borough,p.status,p.document_time_status,r.primary_source_id FROM records r JOIN projects p USING(record_id) WHERE r.record_id IN (<selected aliases and targets>) ORDER BY r.record_id;"
record_existence_and_kind.result: Returned all 14 selected alias/target records as record_kind=project. Selected clusters share project-level names and compatible project_type/project_family/status fields: Fordham Road-Inwood bus_priority, Eastern Parkway B14 transit priority, Utica/B46 SBS, Nostrand/Rogers B44 SBS, First/Second M15 SBS, and Fordham Road Bx12 SBS.

existing_alias_conflicts.merges_json_command: jq -r --argjson aliases '[<selected aliases>]' '.aliases.project | to_entries[] | .key as $k | select($aliases | index($k)) | [.key,.value] | @tsv' data/identity-overrides/merges.json
existing_alias_conflicts.identity_aliases_command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "SELECT kind,alias,target FROM identity_aliases WHERE kind='project' AND alias IN (<selected aliases>);"

do_not_merge_conflicts.db_command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "SELECT kind,record_id_a,record_id_b,reason FROM do_not_merge WHERE kind='project' AND (record_id_a IN (<selected aliases and targets>) OR record_id_b IN (<selected aliases and targets>));"
do_not_merge_conflicts.json_command: jq -r --argjson ids '[<selected aliases and targets>]' '.pairs.project[]? | select(any(.record_ids[]; . as $rid | ($ids | index($rid)))) | [.record_ids[0],.record_ids[1],.reason] | @tsv' data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json

prior_prep_conflicts.command: jq -r --argjson aliases '[<selected aliases>]' '.proposed_aliases[]? | select(.kind=="project") | select(.alias_id as $a | ($aliases | index($a))) | [.kind,.alias_id,.target_id] | @tsv' data/review_notes/codex-identity-batch0{47..99}-prep.json 2>/dev/null | sort -u
prior_prep_conflicts.result: Returned no rows; none of the selected project alias_ids appeared in prior Codex identity prep artifacts through identity099.

source_evidence_commands.command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "SELECT record_id,source_id,block_id,page_number,json_extract(ref_json,'$.source_quote') AS source_quote FROM evidence_refs WHERE record_id IN (<selected aliases and targets>) ORDER BY record_id, ordinal LIMIT 120;"
source_evidence_commands.result: Confirmed citeable evidence refs exist for each selected alias and target record.

source_evidence_commands.command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "SELECT source_id,block_id,substr(replace(replace(raw_text,char(10),' '),char(13),' '),1,320) AS quote FROM blocks_fts WHERE (<selected evidence refs>) ORDER BY source_id,block_id;"
source_evidence_commands.result: Confirmed compact source blocks name the same project scopes: Fordham Road-Inwood, Eastern Parkway B14 from Schenectady to Ralph, Utica/B46 SBS, and the BRT Phase I SBS entries for B44, M15, and Bx12.

scope_and_status_checks.command: sqlite3 -header -csv data/canonical.db "WITH p AS (SELECT r.record_id,r.display_name,r.primary_source_id,COALESCE(NULLIF(p.project_name,''),NULLIF(p.name,''),r.display_name) AS key,p.project_name,p.name,p.project_type,p.project_family,p.borough,p.status,p.document_time_status FROM records r JOIN projects p USING(record_id) WHERE r.record_kind='project'), g AS (SELECT lower(key) k, COUNT(*) n FROM p GROUP BY lower(key) HAVING n>1) SELECT g.n,p.record_id,p.display_name,p.primary_source_id,p.project_name,p.name,p.project_type,p.project_family,p.borough,p.status,p.document_time_status FROM p JOIN g ON lower(p.key)=g.k ORDER BY g.n DESC, g.k, p.record_id LIMIT 200;"
scope_and_status_checks.result: Candidate search was limited to global project rows. Proposed aliases were selected only where document audience, source-time status, route naming, and corridor scope were compatible; broad programs, capital-plan rollups, unrelated same-name street-improvement rows, and ambiguous phase/corridor variants were left out.
